name: Gadget
comment:
This module is for application developers who want to create Gadgets and use
them with pre-existing adapters.

If you want to make your own adapters, see the [Gadget.IR](Gadget-IR) module.


# Gadgets

@docs Gadget


# Primitives

@docs bool, char, string, int, float


# Combinators

@docs list, array, dict, set

@docs tuple, triple

@docs maybe, result


# Records

    import Gadget

    type alias Person =
        { name : String
        , age : Int
        }

    personGadget =
        Gadget.record Person
            |> Gadget.field .name Gadget.string
            |> Gadget.field .age Gadget.int
            |> Gadget.endRecord

    personGadget --: Gadget.Gadget Person

@docs RecordGadgetBuilder, record, field, endRecord


# Custom types

    import Gadget

    type Shape
        = Rectangle Int Int
        | Circle Int

    shapeGadget =
        Gadget.custom
            (\rectangle circle variant ->
                case variant of
                    Rectangle width height ->
                        rectangle width height
                    Circle radius ->
                        circle radius
            )
            |> Gadget.variant2 Rectangle Gadget.int Gadget.int
            |> Gadget.variant1 Circle Gadget.int
            |> Gadget.endCustom

    shapeGadget --: Gadget.Gadget Shape

@docs CustomGadgetBuilder, custom, variant0, variant1, variant2, variant3, variant4, variant5, endCustom


# Transforming Gadgets

@docs map


# Labelling Gadgets

@docs label

unions.name: CustomGadgetBuilder
unions.comment: A type used to build Gadgets for custom types.

unions.name: RecordGadgetBuilder
unions.comment: A type used to build Gadgets for records.

aliases.name: Gadget
aliases.comment:
The core type of this package. Use the primitives and combinators in this
module to define Gadgets for the types in your application.

values.name: array
values.comment:
A combinator used to define Gadgets for arrays.

    import Gadget
    import Array

    arrayGadget =
        Gadget.array Gadget.int

    arrayGadget --: Gadget.Gadget (Array.Array Int)

values.name: bool
values.comment: A Gadget for the `Bool` primitive type.

values.name: char
values.comment: A Gadget for the `Char` primitive type.

values.name: custom
values.comment: Start the definition of a custom type.

values.name: dict
values.comment:
A combinator used to define Gadgets for dictionaries.

    import Gadget
    import Dict

    dictGadget =
        Gadget.dict Gadget.int Gadget.string

    dictGadget --: Gadget.Gadget (Dict.Dict Int String)
values.type: Gadget.Gadget comparable -> Gadget.Gadget v -> Gadget.Gadget (Dict.Dict comparable v)

values.name: endCustom
values.comment: Complete the definition of a custom type.

values.name: endRecord
values.comment: Complete the definition of a record.

values.name: field
values.comment: Add a field to the definition of a record.
values.type: (input -> field) -> Gadget.Gadget field -> Gadget.RecordGadgetBuilder input (field -> output) -> Gadget.RecordGadgetBuilder input output

values.name: float
values.comment: A Gadget for the `Float` primitive type.

values.name: int
values.comment: A Gadget for the `Int` primitive type.

values.name: label
values.comment:
Add a label to a Gadget.

    import Gadget

    labelled =
        Gadget.int
            |> Gadget.label "age"

    labelled --: Gadget.Gadget Int

values.name: list
values.comment:
A combinator used to define Gadgets for lists.

    import Gadget

    listGadget =
        Gadget.list Gadget.int

    listGadget --: Gadget.Gadget (List Int)

values.name: map
values.comment:
Convert a Gadget of one type to a Gadget of another type.

    import Gadget

    charListGadget =
        Gadget.string
            |> Gadget.map
                String.toList
                String.fromList

    charListGadget --: Gadget.Gadget (List Char)
values.type: (a -> b) -> (b -> a) -> Gadget.Gadget a -> Gadget.Gadget b

values.name: maybe
values.comment:
A combinator used to define Gadgets for the Maybe type.

    import Gadget

    maybeGadget =
        Gadget.maybe Gadget.int

    maybeGadget --: Gadget.Gadget (Maybe Int)

values.name: record
values.comment: Start the definition of a record.

values.name: result
values.comment:
A combinator used to define Gadgets for the Result type.

    import Gadget

    resultGadget =
        Gadget.result Gadget.string Gadget.int

    resultGadget --: Gadget.Gadget (Result String Int)
values.type: Gadget.Gadget x -> Gadget.Gadget a -> Gadget.Gadget (Result.Result x a)

values.name: set
values.comment:
A combinator used to define Gadgets for sets.

    import Gadget
    import Set

    dictGadget =
        Gadget.set Gadget.int

    dictGadget --: Gadget.Gadget (Set.Set Int)

values.name: string
values.comment: A Gadget for the `String` primitive type.

values.name: triple
values.comment:
A combinator used to define Gadgets for triples.

    import Gadget

    tripleGadget =
        Gadget.triple Gadget.string Gadget.int Gadget.float

    tripleGadget --: Gadget.Gadget ( String, Int, Float )
values.type: Gadget.Gadget a -> Gadget.Gadget b -> Gadget.Gadget c -> Gadget.Gadget ( a, b, c )

values.name: tuple
values.comment:
A combinator used to define Gadgets for tuples.

    import Gadget

    tupleGadget =
        Gadget.tuple Gadget.string Gadget.int

    tupleGadget --: Gadget.Gadget ( String, Int )
values.type: Gadget.Gadget a -> Gadget.Gadget b -> Gadget.Gadget ( a, b )

values.name: variant0
values.comment: Add a variant with zero arguments to the definition of a custom type.
values.type: output -> Gadget.CustomGadgetBuilder (Gadget.IR.IR -> input) variantType output -> Gadget.CustomGadgetBuilder input () output

values.comment: Add a variant with one argument to the definition of a custom type.
values.type: (arg1 -> output) -> Gadget.Gadget arg1 -> Gadget.CustomGadgetBuilder ((arg1 -> Gadget.IR.IR) -> input) variantType output -> Gadget.CustomGadgetBuilder input () output

values.name: variant2
values.comment: Add a variant with two arguments to the definition of a custom type.
values.type: (arg1 -> arg2 -> output) -> Gadget.Gadget arg1 -> Gadget.Gadget arg2 -> Gadget.CustomGadgetBuilder ((arg1 -> arg2 -> Gadget.IR.IR) -> input) variantType output -> Gadget.CustomGadgetBuilder input () output

values.name: variant3
values.comment: Add a variant with three arguments to the definition of a custom type.
values.type: (arg1 -> arg2 -> arg3 -> output) -> Gadget.Gadget arg1 -> Gadget.Gadget arg2 -> Gadget.Gadget arg3 -> Gadget.CustomGadgetBuilder ((arg1 -> arg2 -> arg3 -> Gadget.IR.IR) -> input) variantType output -> Gadget.CustomGadgetBuilder input () output

values.name: variant4
values.comment: Add a variant with four arguments to the definition of a custom type.
values.type: (arg1 -> arg2 -> arg3 -> arg4 -> output) -> Gadget.Gadget arg1 -> Gadget.Gadget arg2 -> Gadget.Gadget arg3 -> Gadget.Gadget arg4 -> Gadget.CustomGadgetBuilder ((arg1 -> arg2 -> arg3 -> arg4 -> Gadget.IR.IR) -> input) variantType output -> Gadget.CustomGadgetBuilder input () output

values.name: variant5
values.comment: Add a variant with five arguments to the definition of a custom type.
values.type: (arg1 -> arg2 -> arg3 -> arg4 -> arg5 -> output) -> Gadget.Gadget arg1 -> Gadget.Gadget arg2 -> Gadget.Gadget arg3 -> Gadget.Gadget arg4 -> Gadget.Gadget arg5 -> Gadget.CustomGadgetBuilder ((arg1 -> arg2 -> arg3 -> arg4 -> arg5 -> Gadget.IR.IR) -> input) variantType output -> Gadget.CustomGadgetBuilder input () output

name: Gadget.Adapter.Diff
comment:
Generate and use (fairly) efficient diffs between two values of any Elm
type.

    import Gadget
    import Gadget.Adapter.Diff

    gadget =
        Gadget.list Gadget.int

    oldValue =
        [ 1, 2, 3 ]

    newValue =
        [ 1, 2, 3, 4 ]

    changes =
        Gadget.Adapter.Diff.diff gadget oldValue newValue

    changes --: Gadget.Adapter.Diff.Changes

    Gadget.Adapter.Diff.patch gadget changes oldValue

    --> Ok [ 1, 2, 3, 4 ]

@docs Changes, diff, patch

unions.name: Changes
unions.comment: A set of changes that captures the differences between two Elm values.

values.name: diff
values.comment: Compare two values and generate [Changes](#Changes).

values.name: patch
values.comment: Use a set of [Changes](#Changes) to patch a value.
values.type: Gadget.IR.Gadget a -> Gadget.Adapter.Diff.Changes -> a -> Result.Result String.String a

name: Gadget.Adapter.Fuzz
comment:
Generate fuzzers for any Elm type.


# Simple fuzzers

    import Gadget
    import Gadget.Adapter.Fuzz
    import Fuzz

    gadget =
        Gadget.int

    fuzzer =
        Gadget.Adapter.Fuzz.fuzzer gadget

    fuzzer --: Fuzz.Fuzzer Int

    Fuzz.examples 1 fuzzer

    --> [ 105 ]

@docs fuzzer


# Overriding fuzzers

    import Gadget
    import Gadget.Adapter.Fuzz
    import Fuzz

    gadget =
        Gadget.int
            |> Gadget.label "override-me"

    fuzzer =
        Gadget.Adapter.Fuzz.fuzzerWithOverrides
            [ Gadget.Adapter.Fuzz.override "override-me" Gadget.int (Fuzz.constant 3) ]
            gadget

    Fuzz.examples 1 fuzzer

    --> [ 3 ]

@docs fuzzerWithOverrides, Override, override

name: Gadget.IR

unions.name: Gadget
unions.comment:
The core type of this package. Use the functions in this module together
with an appropriate Gadget to convert values to and from the IR type.
cases: { fromInput : a -> Gadget.IR.IR, toOutput : Gadget.IR.IR -> Result.Result Gadget.IR.Error a, irType : Gadget.IR.IRType }

unions.name: IR
unions.comment:
IR values are variants of this type. All Elm values (as long as they don't
contain functions) should be able to be encoded as a value of this type.

unions.name: IRType
unions.comment: Any IR value will have a "type" that is a variant of IRType.

unions.name: Variant
unions.comment: A type used by the `Custom` constructor of the IR type.

unions.name: VariantType
unions.comment: A type used by the `Custom` constructor of the IRType type.

aliases.name: Error
aliases.comment: An error that may be generated if `toOutput` fails.

values.name: fromInput
values.comment: Use an appropriate Gadget to convert an Elm value into an IR value.

values.name: irType
values.comment: Use an appropriate Gadget to extract the IRType of an Elm value.

values.name: toOutput
values.comment:
Use an appropriate Gadget to attempt to convert an IR value into an Elm
value.
values.type: Gadget.IR.Gadget a -> Gadget.IR.IR -> Result.Result Gadget.IR.Error a
